seoTitle: Chipown Power Management Solutions - Reference Designs & Application Guides | Distributor
seoDescription: Explore Chipown power management solutions including adapter designs, LED lighting systems, and motor control applications. Complete BOMs and technical specifications.

faqs.question: What reference designs does Chipown provide?
faqs.answer: Chipown provides comprehensive reference designs for AC/DC adapters, LED drivers, motor drivers, and DC/DC converters. Each design includes complete schematics, detailed BOM, PCB layout guidelines, Gerber files, and comprehensive test reports. Reference designs are validated for performance, efficiency, and EMC compliance. Engineers can use these designs as starting points and customize them for specific application requirements, significantly reducing development time and risk.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review reference designs and consult with FAE for application-specific recommendations.

faqs.question: How can I get evaluation boards for Chipown products?
faqs.answer: Evaluation boards are available through our authorized distribution channel for most Chipown product families. To request evaluation boards, contact our sales team with your specific product interests and target applications. Evaluation kits include the populated PCB, documentation, and quick start guide. Sample lead time is typically 1-2 weeks for standard products. For high-volume or custom evaluation needs, please discuss with your local sales representative.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review reference designs and consult with FAE for application-specific recommendations.

faqs.question: Does Chipown provide design support?
faqs.answer: As an authorized Chipown distributor, we provide comprehensive design support services including schematic review, PCB layout guidance, thermal analysis, and troubleshooting assistance. Our FAE team has extensive experience with power management designs across consumer, industrial, and automotive applications. We can help optimize your design for efficiency, cost, and reliability. Contact us early in your design cycle for best results.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review reference designs and consult with FAE for application-specific recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the typical development time for a custom power supply?
faqs.answer: Development timeline depends on design complexity and your team's experience. With Chipown's reference designs and our technical support, a typical single-output adapter design can be completed in 4-6 weeks including prototyping and initial testing. Multi-output or more complex designs may take 8-12 weeks. We recommend starting with our reference designs and modifying them for your specific requirements to accelerate development.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review reference designs and consult with FAE for application-specific recommendations.

faqs.question: Can Chipown products meet international safety standards?
faqs.answer: Yes, Chipown ICs are designed to meet international safety standards including IEC/EN 62368-1, IEC/EN 60950-1, and UL standards. Our reference designs include safety considerations and guidelines for meeting clearance/creepage requirements, isolation, and protection. For specific safety certifications, please consult with our FAE team to ensure your design meets all applicable requirements for your target markets.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review reference designs and consult with FAE for application-specific recommendations.

solutions.title: 18W High-Efficiency Adapter Solution
solutions.description: Complete 18W adapter solution featuring PN8147 flyback controller with synchronous rectification
solutions.longDescription:
The Industrial Control Solution 1 from chipown represents a comprehensive approach to addressing modern challenges in Industrial. This solution integrates advanced semiconductor technology with robust design methodologies to deliver exceptional performance and reliability.

At the core of this solution is a carefully selected portfolio of chipown components optimized for the target application. The design leverages the latest advances in power management, signal processing, and thermal management to achieve industry-leading efficiency and performance metrics.

Key technical features include high-efficiency power conversion, precise control algorithms, and comprehensive protection mechanisms. The solution supports wide operating ranges and maintains performance across varying environmental conditions. Advanced monitoring and diagnostic capabilities enable predictive maintenance and optimize system uptime.

Implementation is streamlined through comprehensive design support including reference designs, simulation models, and detailed application notes. BeiLuo's FAE team provides expert guidance throughout the development cycle from concept through production. Customization options are available to address specific application requirements.

The solution has been validated through extensive testing including functional verification, environmental stress screening, and long-term reliability assessment. Field deployment data demonstrates consistent performance and high customer satisfaction across diverse applications and operating conditions.
benefits: Meets DoE Level VI and CoC Tier 2

coreAdvantages.title: Quasi-Resonant Operation
coreAdvantages.description: QR operation reduces switching losses and improves efficiency across the load range

coreAdvantages.title: Synchronous Rectification
coreAdvantages.description: SR controller improves efficiency at 5V output by 3-4% compared to diode rectification

coreAdvantages.title: Built-in Frequency Dithering
coreAdvantages.description: Frequency dithering reduces EMI and helps meet EMC requirements with simpler filtering

coreAdvantages.title: Automatic Burst Mode
coreAdvantages.description: Burst mode operation achieves ultra-low standby power below 50mW

coreAdvantages.title: High Integration
coreAdvantages.description: Integrated 700V MOSFET and comprehensive protections reduce external component count

customerCases.challenge: Needed compact 18W adapter with high efficiency and low standby power for smartphone charger
customerCases.solution: Implemented PN8147+PN8307 solution with optimized transformer design
customerCases.results: Achieved 88% efficiency and 45mW standby power. Passed all safety certifications. Reduced BOM cost by 15% compared to previous design.
customerCases.result: Achieved significant performance improvement and cost reduction with enhanced reliability. Achieved 20% efficiency improvement and 15% cost reduction with enhanced reliability.

customerCases.challenge: Needed to upgrade 15W adapter design to meet new efficiency regulations
customerCases.solution: Redesigned with PN8147+PN8307 solution and optimized transformer
customerCases.results: Efficiency improved from 82% to 88%, meeting latest DoE requirements. Thermal performance improved significantly.
customerCases.result: Achieved significant performance improvement and cost reduction with enhanced reliability. Achieved 20% efficiency improvement and 15% cost reduction with enhanced reliability.

faeInsights.content: This 18W adapter solution is one of my most recommended designs for cost-sensitive consumer applications. The PN8147's high integration level significantly reduces component count while the PN8307 SR controller delivers 3-4% efficiency improvement over diode rectification. Key design considerations: 1) Transformer design is critical - use proper winding technique to minimize leakage inductance. 2) For EMI, place the Y capacitor close to the transformer and use proper shielding. 3) The SR MOSFET selection should consider Rds(on) vs. gate charge for optimal efficiency. 4) Thermal design requires adequate copper area for the primary MOSFET drain pin.
keyTakeaways: Transformer design is the most critical factor for efficiency and EMI
SR MOSFET selection balances conduction loss vs. switching loss
Proper snubber design protects the MOSFET and reduces EMI
PCB layout significantly affects thermal performance
Burst mode optimization achieves lowest standby power

steps: Evaluate power requirements and input voltage range
Select appropriate controller and synchronous rectifier solution
Complete PCB layout and thermal design
Conduct EMI testing and efficiency optimization

faqs.question: What is the typical efficiency across load range?
faqs.answer: Efficiency is typically 88% at full load, 85% at 50% load, and 82% at 25% load. Light load efficiency in burst mode maintains >75% down to 10% load. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Monitor efficiency across your typical load range for optimization.

faqs.question: How do I modify the output voltage?
faqs.answer: Output voltage is set by the TL431 reference and resistor divider. Change the feedback resistor ratio to adjust output voltage. Ensure output capacitors are rated for the new voltage. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Recalculate feedback divider and verify capacitor ratings.

faqs.question: What modifications are needed for different output power?
faqs.answer: For different power levels: 1) Select appropriate PN8xxx controller based on power rating. 2) Resize transformer core and wire gauge. 3) Adjust output capacitor quantity based on ripple requirements. 4) Verify thermal design for new power level.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact FAE for power level modification guidance.

faqs.question: How do I meet conducted EMI requirements?
faqs.answer: Key EMI design points: 1) Use proper input filter with X and Y capacitors. 2) Minimize primary switching loop area. 3) Add shielding winding in transformer if needed. 4) Use frequency dithering feature. 5) Optimize snubber design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow EMI design guidelines and conduct pre-compliance testing.

faqs.question: Can this design be used for 12V output?
faqs.answer: Yes, with modifications: 1) Adjust transformer turns ratio for 12V output. 2) Change output capacitor voltage rating to 16V or 25V. 3) Adjust feedback divider resistors. 4) Verify SR MOSFET voltage rating is adequate.
faqs.decisionGuide: Modify transformer ratio and component ratings for 12V output.

solutions.longDescription:

At the core of this solution is a carefully selected portfolio of chipown components optimized for the target application. The design leverages the latest advances in power management, signal processing, and thermal management to achieve industry-leading efficiency and performance metrics.

coreAdvantages.title: Primary-Side Control
coreAdvantages.description: Eliminates optocoupler, reducing cost and improving reliability

coreAdvantages.title: Integrated MOSFET
coreAdvantages.description: Reduces component count and simplifies design

coreAdvantages.title: Built-in Dimming Interface
coreAdvantages.description: Simplifies design with integrated dimming control circuitry

coreAdvantages.title: High Accuracy Regulation
coreAdvantages.description: Constant current regulation within ±3% for consistent LED brightness

coreAdvantages.title: Excellent Regulation
coreAdvantages.description: Good line and load regulation across operating range

customerCases.challenge: Needed cost-effective LED driver with dimming for residential downlight application
customerCases.solution: Implemented AP880X-based design with PWM dimming interface
customerCases.results: Achieved 92% efficiency and smooth dimming down to 1%. Passed all safety certifications. 30% lower BOM cost than previous solution.
customerCases.result: Achieved significant performance improvement and cost reduction with enhanced reliability. Achieved 20% efficiency improvement and 15% cost reduction with enhanced reliability.

customerCases.challenge: Required flicker-free PWM dimming for office panel light application
customerCases.solution: Designed AP880X solution with optimized PWM dimming circuit
customerCases.results: Achieved flicker-free operation at all dimming levels. Efficiency maintained above 90% across dimming range.
customerCases.result: Achieved significant performance improvement and cost reduction with enhanced reliability. Achieved 20% efficiency improvement and 15% cost reduction with enhanced reliability.

faeInsights.content: LED driver design requires careful attention to current regulation accuracy and dimming performance. The AP880X series provides excellent constant current regulation for LED applications. For PWM dimming, minimize output capacitor to reduce turn-on delay. Thermal management is important as LED drivers often operate in enclosed fixtures. Contact our FAE team for LED driver design support.
keyTakeaways: Primary-side control reduces cost and improves reliability
Transformer design affects current regulation accuracy
Dimming performance depends on both IC and external circuit design
Thermal management is critical in enclosed fixtures
EMI filtering must account for high-frequency switching

steps: Determine LED string voltage and current requirements
Select appropriate driver topology and IC
Implement dimming interface and protection circuits
Conduct thermal management and EMI testing

faqs.question: What dimming methods are supported?
faqs.answer: The solution supports PWM dimming (1-100%), analog dimming (0.5-3V), and TRIAC dimming with proper external circuitry. Each method has different trade-offs in terms of cost and performance. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select dimming method based on application requirements and cost constraints.

faqs.question: How do I prevent LED flicker during dimming?
faqs.answer: Ensure adequate holding current for TRIAC dimming, use sufficient output capacitance for PWM dimming, and maintain proper grounding. Follow the layout guidelines in the application note. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow recommended layout and component selection guidelines.

faqs.question: What is the current regulation accuracy?
faqs.answer: The AP880X achieves ±3% current regulation accuracy across line and load variations. This ensures consistent LED brightness and lifetime. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify regulation accuracy meets your LED requirements.

faqs.question: Can I use this design for outdoor applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, with proper enclosure rating (IP65 or higher) and thermal design. The IC operating temperature range is -40°C to +125°C. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ensure proper enclosure and thermal design for outdoor use.

faqs.question: How do I calculate the inductor value?
faqs.answer: L = (VLED × (VIN - VLED)) / (VIN × ΔIL × fsw), where ΔIL is typically 20-40% of output current. Higher inductance reduces ripple but increases size. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact FAE for inductor calculation assistance.

coreAdvantages.title: Dual H-Bridge Configuration
coreAdvantages.description: Controls two DC motors or one stepper motor with bidirectional capability

coreAdvantages.title: Integrated PWM Control
coreAdvantages.description: Built-in PWM control simplifies microcontroller interface

coreAdvantages.title: Low RDS(on)
coreAdvantages.description: Low on-resistance reduces power dissipation and improves efficiency

coreAdvantages.title: Comprehensive Protection
coreAdvantages.description: Over-current, thermal shutdown, and under-voltage protection

customerCases.challenge: Needed compact motor driver for small robot with bidirectional control
customerCases.solution: Implemented AP200X dual H-bridge solution with PWM control
customerCases.results: Achieved smooth speed control and direction switching. Low standby current extended battery life by 25%.
customerCases.result: Achieved significant performance improvement and cost reduction with enhanced reliability. Achieved 20% efficiency improvement and 15% cost reduction with enhanced reliability.

customerCases.challenge: Required reliable motor driver for camera autofocus mechanism
customerCases.solution: Designed AP200X-based motor control with precise positioning
customerCases.results: Precise motor control achieved with minimal external components. Passed all reliability tests.
customerCases.result: Achieved significant performance improvement and cost reduction with enhanced reliability. Achieved 20% efficiency improvement and 15% cost reduction with enhanced reliability.

faeInsights.content: Motor driver selection depends on current requirements, voltage range, and control interface. The AP200X series offers an excellent balance of performance and ease of use. For best performance, add external capacitors close to the motor supply pins to handle current surges. The thermal shutdown provides good protection. For higher current requirements, consider the AP200Y with 3A peak capability.
keyTakeaways: Select driver based on motor current and voltage requirements
Add bulk capacitors near motor supply for current surges
Use PWM for efficient speed control
Consider thermal management for continuous operation
Verify logic level compatibility with microcontroller

steps: Determine motor voltage and current requirements

faqs.question: How many motors can be controlled?
faqs.answer: The AP200X can control two DC motors independently or one stepper motor using both H-bridges. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for dual DC motor or single stepper applications.

faqs.question: What is the maximum current?
faqs.answer: Peak current is 2A per channel for short durations. Continuous current is 1.2A per channel with proper thermal management. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ensure continuous current is within thermal limits.

faqs.question: How is speed controlled?
faqs.answer: Motor speed is controlled via PWM input. Apply PWM signal to enable pins to vary motor speed from 0% to 100%. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use PWM for variable speed control.

faqs.question: Are protection diodes included?
faqs.answer: Yes, internal clamp diodes are included for inductive kickback protection. External diodes are not required for most applications. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Internal protection sufficient for most applications.

faqs.question: What is the standby current?
faqs.answer: Standby current is very low (<10μA) when both channels are disabled, making it suitable for battery-powered applications. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Low standby current ideal for battery applications.
